name: Darul Qasim
mission: TO FACILITATE THE LEARNING AND IMPARTING OF KNOKLEDGE DERIVED FROM PROPHETHOOD OF MUHAMMAD , ARABIC LANGUAGE AND RELIGIOUS EDUCATION
theoryOfChange: By re-presenting the Islamic intellectual tradition with clarity and academic rigor, the organization aims to produce scholars capable of navigating modern challenges and inspiring conviction in the Truth.
scoreSummary: Darul Qasim shows moderate alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 29 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 7.8% of revenue ($209,869 on $2,696,418)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions a "stable financial foundation" as a strength, but the risk section identifies a medium risk related to holding 29 months of reserves, which contradicts the idea of a stable financial foundation. This could be clarified.
The impact rationale mentions a healthy 29.3 months of working capital, but the financial health component in the score details only scored 3/7. This discrepancy could be clarified.

all_citations.claim: The charity reported total revenue of $2,696,418, a program expense ratio of 51.9%, and fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised.
The organization holds 29.3 months of working capital.
Darul Qasim is zakat-eligible and maintains a specific zakat policy for its programs.
The organization provides specialized religious education including the Iftā Program and Takhaṣṣuṣ fī al-Tafsīr.
Darul Qasim's programs are designed to facilitate learning derived from the Prophethood of Muhammad.
The institution focuses on the intersection of Islam and education to preserve religious knowledge.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Darul Qasim earns a moderate score of 50/100, reflecting a stable financial foundation and clear religious mission balanced by a lower program expense ratio and a lack of independent impact verification.
areas_for_improvement: program expense ratio of 51.9% suggests a significant portion of funds is directed toward administrative or other non-program costs
Limited third-party impact data regarding long-term beneficiary outcomes

dimension_explanations.alignment: Darul Qasim shows medium alignment for Muslim donors by offering a zakat-eligible pathway for supporting global religious education [3][7]. Its curriculum is rooted in traditional Islamic epistemology, though the urgency of the cause is categorized as general education rather than immediate humanitarian relief [4][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through its Iftā and Takhaṣṣuṣ fī al-Tafsīr programs [4][5]. While it maintains a healthy 29.3 months of working capital, the program expense ratio is 51.9%, indicating that just over half of its $2,696,418 revenue is spent directly on its core educational initiatives [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Darul Qasim is an Islamic institution of higher learning dedicated to preserving and imparting traditional knowledge derived from the Prophetic tradition.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with only $0.02 spent to raise every $1
Strong liquidity position with 29.3 months of working capital available
Clear zakat policy and eligibility for donor contributions
baseline_narrative.summary: Based in Illinois, Darul Qasim facilitates the study of the Arabic language and religious education through specialized academic tracks [1][4]. The organization reported a total revenue of $2,696,418 and maintains a zakat-eligible status to support its educational mission [1][3].

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="7">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite>, which is significantly better than industry averages.
Darul Qasim maintains <cite id="7">29.3 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring long-term operational stability.
The institution offers unique programs such as <cite id="4">Takhaṣṣuṣ fī al-Tafsīr</cite> and Islamic Theology.

strengths_deep_dive: Darul Qasim excels in financial sustainability and specialized curriculum design. Its <cite id="7">100% independent board</cite> provides strong oversight of its <cite id="2">$10.2 million in total assets</cite>. The institution's ability to generate <cite id="2">$835,237 in program service revenue</cite> suggests a diversified income stream beyond just donations. This earned income from tuition or services enhances its institutional maturity.
rich_narrative.summary: Darul Qasim is an Illinois-based institution of higher learning dedicated to preserving traditional Islamic knowledge. The organization focuses on <cite id="1">Arabic language and religious education</cite> through specialized academic tracks. These include the <cite id="4">Iftā Program and Advanced Islamic Law</cite>. The institution reported <cite id="2">$3,290,091 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. However, the most recent data shows a <cite id="2">revenue decline to $2,696,418</cite> in 2024. Darul Qasim maintains <cite id="3">zakat-eligible status</cite> to support its educational mission. The school operates with a <cite id="2">program expense ratio of 51.9%</cite>. This indicates that a significant portion of funds supports administrative functions rather than direct educational delivery. Despite this, the organization shows <cite id="7">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only $0.02 to raise every $1. This lean fundraising model allows more resources to remain within the institution. The organization also maintains a <cite id="7">strong liquidity position</cite> with over 29.3 months of working capital. This financial cushion provides stability during periods of revenue fluctuation.
